name: ovos-bus-client

metadata.name: ovos-bus-client
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# OpenVoiceOS Bus Client

This module is a simple interface for the OVOS messagebus and can be used to connect to OVOS, send messages and react to messages sent by the OpenVoiceOS system.


## MycroftBusClient()

The `MycroftBusClient()` object can be setup to connect to any host and port as well as any endpont on that host. this makes it quite versitile and will work on the main bus as well as on a gui bus. If no arguments are provided it will try to connect to a local instance of OVOS on the default endpoint and port.

> NOTE: we kept the original pre-fork class name for compatibility reasons

## Message()

The `Message` object is a representation of the messagebus message, this will always contain a message type but can also contain data and context. Data is usually real information while the context typically contain information on where the message originated or who the intended recipient is.

```python
Message('MESSAGE_TYPE', data={'meaning': 42}, context={'origin': 'A.Dent'})
```

## Examples

Below are some a couple of simple cases for sending a message on the bus as well
as reacting to messages on the bus

### Sending a message on the bus.

```python
from ovos_bus_client import MessageBusClient, Message

print('Setting up client to connect to a local OVOS instance')
client = MessageBusClient()
client.run_in_thread()

print('Sending speak message...')
client.emit(Message('speak', data={'utterance': 'Hello World'}))
```

### Catching a message on the messagebus

```python
from ovos_bus_client import MessageBusClient, Message

print('Setting up client to connect to a local OVOS instance')
client = MessageBusClient()

def print_utterance(message):
    print('OVOS said "{}"'.format(message.data.get('utterance')))


print('Registering handler for speak message...')
client.on('speak', print_utterance)

client.run_forever()
```

functions.name: ovos_bus_client.send_func.send
documentation:
Send a single message over the websocket.

Args:
    message_to_send (str): Message to send
    data_to_send (dict): data structure to go along with the
        message, defaults to empty dict.

documentation: Receive response data.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.waiter.MessageWaiter.wait
documentation:
Wait for message.

Arguments:
    timeout (int or float): seconds to wait for message

Returns:
    Message or None

Wait for a single message.

Encapsulate the wait for a message logic separating the setup from
the actual waiting act so the waiting can be setup, actions can be
performed and _then_ the message can be waited for.

Arguments:
    bus: Bus to check for messages on
    message_type: message type(s) to wait for

documentation:
Send collection request.

Register handler to capture handlers trying to provide answer.

documentation: Emit message and wait for handlers to finish.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.collector.MessageCollector.wait
documentation: Wait for timeout or for all handlers to respond.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.collector.MessageCollector._wait_for_registered_handlers
documentation: Wait until all handlers have sent a response or the timeout is reached.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.collector.MessageCollector.shutdown
documentation: Shutdown the object, stop waiting for further responses.

Collect multiple response.

This class encapsulates the logic for collecting messages from
multiple handlers returning the list of all answers.

Argunments:
    bus: Bus to check for messages on
    message (Message): message to send
    min_timeout (int/float): Minimum time to wait for a response
    max_timeout (int/float): Maximum allowed time to wait for an answer
    direct_return_func (callable): Optional function for allowing an
        early return (not all registered handlers need to respond)

documentation: Setup websocket client.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.on_open
documentation:
Handle the "open" event from the websocket.
A Basic message with the name "open" is forwarded to the emitter.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.on_close
documentation:
Handle the "close" event from the websocket.
A Basic message with the name "close" is forwarded to the emitter.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.on_error
documentation: On error start trying to reconnect to the websocket.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.on_message
ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.emit
documentation:
Send a message onto the message bus.

This will both send the message to the local process using the
event emitter and onto the Mycroft websocket for other processes.

Args:
    message (Message): Message to send

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.collect_responses
documentation:
Collect responses from multiple handlers.

This sets up a collect-call (pun intended) expecting multiple handlers
to respond.

Args:
    message (Message): message to send
    min_timeout (int/float): Minimum time to wait for a response
    max_timeout (int/float): Maximum allowed time to wait for an answer
    direct_return_func (callable): Optional function for allowing an
        early return (not all registered handlers need to respond)

    Returns:
        (list) collected response messages.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.on_collect
documentation:
Create a handler for a collect_responses call.

This immeditely responds with an ack to register the handler with
the caller, promising to return a response.

The handler function then needs to send a response.

Args:
    event_name (str): Message type to listen for.
    func (callable): function / method do be called for processing the
                     message.
    timeout (int/float): optional timeout of the handler

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.wait_for_message
documentation:
Wait for a message of a specific type.

Arguments:
    message_type (str): the message type of the expected message
    timeout: seconds to wait before timeout, defaults to 3

Returns:
    The received message or None if the response timed out

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.wait_for_response
documentation:
Send a message and wait for a response.

Arguments:
    message (Message): message to send
    reply_type (str | List[str]): the message type(s) of the expected reply.
                      Defaults to "<message.msg_type>.response".
    timeout: seconds to wait before timeout, defaults to 3

Returns:
    The received message or None if the response timed out

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.on
documentation:
Register callback with event emitter.

Args:
    event_name (str): message type to map to the callback
    func (callable): callback function

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.once
documentation:
Register callback with event emitter for a single call.

Args:
    event_name (str): message type to map to the callback
    func (callable): callback function

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.remove

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.remove_all_listeners

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.run_forever

documentation: Close the websocket connection.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.MessageBusClient.run_in_thread
documentation: Launches the run_forever in a separate daemon thread.

The Mycroft Messagebus Client

The Messagebus client connects to the Mycroft messagebus service
and allows communication with the system. It has been extended to work
like the pyee EventEmitter and tries to offer as much convenience as
possible to the developer.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.client.client.GUIWebsocketClient.emit

Send a message onto the message bus.

This will both send the message to the local process using the
event emitter and onto the Mycroft websocket for other processes.

Args:

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.session.IntentContextManagerFrame.__init__
documentation:
Manages entities and context for a single frame of conversation.
Provides simple equality querying.
Attributes:
    entities(list): Entities that belong to ContextManagerFrame
    metadata(object): metadata to describe context belonging to ContextManagerFrame

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.session.IntentContextManagerFrame.serialize
documentation: Get a dict representation of this frame

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.session.IntentContextManagerFrame.deserialize
ovos_bus_client.session.IntentContextManagerFrame.metadata_matches
documentation:
Returns key matches to metadata
Asserts that the contents of query exist within (logical subset of)
metadata in this frame.
Args:
    query(dict): metadata for matching
Returns:
    bool:
        True: when key count in query is > 0 and all keys in query in
            self.metadata
        False: if key count in query is <= 0 or any key in query not
            found in self.metadata

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.session.IntentContextManagerFrame.merge_context
documentation:
merge into contextManagerFrame new entity and metadata.
Appends tag as new entity and adds keys in metadata to keys in
self.metadata.
Args:
    tag(dict): entity to be added to self.entities
    metadata(dict): metadata contains keys to be added to self.metadata

Add context to the first frame in the stack. If no frame metadata

Constructs a list of entities from the context.

Args:

    missing_entities(list of str): a list or set of tag names,
    as strings

Returns:

Context Manager

Use to track context throughout the course of a conversational session.
How to manage a session's lifecycle is not captured here.

modules.name: ovos_bus_client.conf
documentation:
Message bus configuration loader.

The message bus event handler and client use basically the same configuration.
This code is re-used in both to load config values.

functions.name: ovos_bus_client.conf.load_message_bus_config
documentation:
Load the bits of device configuration needed to run the message bus.
@param overrides: Optional config overrides
    host (str): messagebus host
    port (int): messagebus port
    route (str): messagebus route
    ssl (bool): enable SSL on websocket
@return: MessageBusConfig with valid configuration

functions.name: ovos_bus_client.conf.load_gui_message_bus_config
documentation:
Load the bits of device configuration needed to run the GUI bus.
@param overrides: Optional config overrides
    host (str): GUI ebus host
    port (int): GUI bus port
    route (str): GUI bus route
    ssl (bool): enable SSL on websocket
@return: MessageBusConfig with valid configuration

functions.name: ovos_bus_client.conf.client_from_config

documentation:
Continuously listens and reacts to a specific messagetype on the mycroft messagebus

NOTE: when finished you should call bus.remove(msg_type, handler)

documentation: listens and reacts once to a specific messagetype on the mycroft messagebus

functions.name: ovos_bus_client.util.wait_for_reply
documentation:
Send a message and wait for a response.

Args:
    message (FakeMessage or str or dict): message object or type to send
    reply_type (str): the message type of the expected reply.
                      Defaults to "<message.type>.response".
    timeout: seconds to wait before timeout, defaults to 3
Returns:
    The received message or None if the response timed out

modules.name: ovos_bus_client.message
documentation:
Classes and functions related to the Mycroft Message.

The Message object is the core construct passed on the message bus
it contains methods for tracking message context and
serializing / deserializing the message for transmission.
Copyright 2017 Mycroft AI Inc.
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

limitations under the License.

functions.name: ovos_bus_client.message.dig_for_message
documentation:
Dig Through the stack for message. Looks at the current stack
for a passed argument of type 'Message'.
Args:
    max_records (int): Maximum number of stack records to look through

Returns:
    Message if found in args, else None

classes.name: ovos_bus_client.message.Message

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.Message.__init__
documentation:
Used to construct a message object

Message objects will be used to send information back and forth
between processes of mycroft service, voice, skill and cli

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.Message.serialize
documentation:
This returns a string of the message info.

This makes it easy to send over a websocket. This uses
json dumps to generate the string with type, data and context

Returns:
    str: a json string representation of the message.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.Message.deserialize
documentation:
This takes a string and constructs a message object.

This makes it easy to take strings from the websocket and create
a message object.  This uses json loads to get the info and generate
the message object.

Args:
    value(str): This is the json string received from the websocket

Returns:
    Message: message object constructed from the json string passed
    int the function.
    value(str): This is the string received from the websocket

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.Message.forward
documentation:
Keep context and forward message

This will take the same parameters as a message object but use
the current message object as a reference.  It will copy the context
from the existing message object.

Args:
    msg_type (str): type of message
    data (dict): data for message

Returns:
    Message: Message object to be used on the reply to the message

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.Message.reply
documentation:
Construct a reply message for a given message

This will take the same parameters as a message object but use
the current message object as a reference.  It will copy the context
from the existing message object and add any context passed in to
the function.  Check for a destination passed in to the function from
the data object and add that to the context as a destination.  If the
context has a source then that will be swapped with the destination
in the context.  The new message will then have data passed in plus the
new context generated.

Args:
    msg_type (str): type of message
    data (dict): data for message
    context: intended context for new message

Returns:
    Message: Message object to be used on the reply to the message

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.Message.response
documentation:
Construct a response message for the message

Constructs a reply with the data and appends the expected
".response" to the message

Args:
    data (dict): message data
    context (dict): message context
Returns
    (Message) message with the type modified to match default response

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.Message.publish
documentation:
Copy the original context and add passed in context.  Delete
any target in the new context. Return a new message object with
passed in data and new context.  Type remains unchanged.

Args:
    msg_type (str): type of message
    data (dict): data to send with message
    context: context added to existing context

Returns:
    Message: Message object to publish

Holds and manipulates data sent over the websocket

    Message objects will be used to send information back and forth
    between processes of Mycroft.

Attributes:
    msg_type (str): type of data sent within the message.
    data (dict): data sent within the message
    context: info about the message not part of data such as source,
        destination or domain.

classes.name: ovos_bus_client.message.CollectionMessage

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.CollectionMessage.from_message
documentation:
Build a CollectionMessage based of a Message object.

Args:
    message (Message): the original message
    handler_id (str): the handler_id of the recipient
    query_id (str): the query session id

Returns:
    CollectionMessage based on the original Message object

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.CollectionMessage.success
documentation:
Create a message indicating a successful result.

The handler could handle the query and created some sort of response.
The source and destination is switched in the context like when
sending a normal response message.

    data (dict): message data
    context (dict): message context
Returns:
    Message

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.CollectionMessage.failure
documentation:
Create a message indicating a failing result.

The handler could not handle the query.
The source and destination is switched in the context like when
sending a normal response message.

    data (dict): message data
    context (dict): message context
Returns:
    Message

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.CollectionMessage.extend
documentation:
Extend current timeout,

The timeout provided will be added to the existing timeout.
The source and destination is switched in the context like when
sending a normal response message.

Arguments:
    timeout (int/float): timeout extension

Returns:
    Extension message.

Extension of the Message class for use with collect handlers.

The class provides the convenience methods success and failure to report
these states back to the origin.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.message.GUIMessage.serialize
documentation:
This returns a string of the message info.

This makes it easy to send over a websocket. This uses
json dumps to generate the string with type, data and context

Returns:
    str: a json string representation of the message.

modules.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler
documentation:
The scheduler module allows setting up scheduled messages.

A scheduled message will be kept and not sent until the system clock time
criteria is met.
Copyright 2019 Mycroft AI Inc.
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

functions.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.repeat_time
documentation:
Next scheduled time for repeating event. Guarantees that the
time is not in the past (but could skip interim events)

Args:
    sched_time (float): Scheduled unix time for the event
    repeat (float):     Repeat period in seconds

Returns: (float) time for next event

classes.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.is_running
documentation: Return True while scheduler is running

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.load
documentation: Load json data with active events from json file.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.run
documentation: Check events periodically until stopped

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.check_state
documentation: Check if any event should be triggered.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.schedule_event
documentation:
Add event to pending event schedule.

Arguments:
    event (str): Handler for the event
    sched_time (float): epoch time of event
    repeat ([type], optional): Defaults to None. [description]
    data ([type], optional): Defaults to None. [description]
    context (dict, optional): context (dict, optional): message
                              context to send when the
                              handler is called

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.schedule_event_handler
documentation:
Messagebus interface to the schedule_event method.
Required data in the message envelope is
    event: event to emit
    time:  time to emit the event

Optional data is
    repeat: repeat interval
    data:   data to send along with the event

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.remove_event
documentation:
Remove an event from the list of scheduled events.

Arguments:
    event (str): event identifier

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.remove_event_handler
documentation: Messagebus interface to the remove_event method.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.update_event
documentation:
Change an existing event's data.

This will only update the first call if multiple calls are registered
to the same event identifier.

Arguments:
    event (str): event identifier
    data (dict): new data

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.update_event_handler
documentation: Messagebus interface to the update_event method.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.get_event_handler
documentation:
Messagebus interface to get_event.

Emits another event sending event status.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.store
documentation: Write current schedule to disk.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.clear_repeating
documentation: Remove repeating events from events dict.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.clear_empty
documentation: Remove empty event entries from events dict.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.util.scheduler.EventScheduler.shutdown
documentation: Stop the running thread.

Create an event scheduler thread. Will send messages at a
predetermined time to the registered targets.

Arguments:
    bus:            Mycroft messagebus client
    schedule_file:  filename used to store pending events to on
                    shutdown. File is created in XDG_DATA_HOME
    autostart: if True, start scheduler on init

classes.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.register
documentation:
Registers a skill as active. Used for speak() and speak_dialog()
to 'patch' a previous implementation. Somewhat hacky.
DEPRECATED - unused

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.reset
documentation:
The enclosure should restore itself to a started state.
Typically this would be represented by the eyes being 'open'
and the mouth reset to its default (smile or blank).

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.system_reset
documentation: The enclosure hardware should reset any CPUs, etc.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.system_mute
documentation: Mute (turn off) the system speaker.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.system_unmute
documentation: Unmute (turn on) the system speaker.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.system_blink
documentation:
The 'eyes' should blink the given number of times.
Args:
    times (int): number of times to blink

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_on
documentation: Illuminate or show the eyes.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_off
documentation: Turn off or hide the eyes.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_blink
documentation:
Make the eyes blink
Args:
    side (str): 'r', 'l', or 'b' for 'right', 'left' or 'both'

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_narrow
documentation: Make the eyes look narrow, like a squint

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_look
documentation:
Make the eyes look to the given side
Args:
    side (str): 'r' for right
                'l' for left
                'u' for up
                'd' for down
                'c' for crossed

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_color
documentation:
Change the eye color to the given RGB color
Args:
    r (int): 0-255, red value
    g (int): 0-255, green value
    b (int): 0-255, blue value

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_setpixel
documentation:
Set individual pixels of the Mark 1 neopixel eyes
Args:
    idx (int): 0-11 for the right eye, 12-23 for the left
    r (int): The red value to apply
    g (int): The green value to apply
    b (int): The blue value to apply

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_fill
documentation:
Use the eyes as a type of progress meter
Args:
    percentage (int): 0-49 fills the right eye, 50-100 also covers left

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_brightness
documentation:
Set the brightness of the eyes in the display.
Args:
    level (int): 1-30, bigger numbers being brighter

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_reset
documentation: Restore the eyes to their default (ready) state.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_spin
documentation: Make the eyes 'roll'

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_timed_spin
documentation:
Make the eyes 'roll' for the given time.
Args:
    length (int): duration in milliseconds of roll, None = forever

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.eyes_volume
documentation:
Indicate the volume using the eyes
Args:
    volume (int): 0 to 11

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.mouth_reset
documentation: Restore the mouth display to normal (blank)

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.mouth_talk
documentation: Show a generic 'talking' animation for non-synched speech

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.mouth_think
documentation: Show a 'thinking' image or animation

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.mouth_listen
documentation: Show a 'thinking' image or animation

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.mouth_smile
documentation: Show a 'smile' image or animation

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.mouth_viseme
documentation:
Send mouth visemes as a list in a single message.

Args:
    start (int):    Timestamp for start of speech
    viseme_pairs:   Pairs of viseme id and cumulative end times
                    (code, end time)

                    codes:
                     0 = shape for sounds like 'y' or 'aa'
                     1 = shape for sounds like 'aw'
                     2 = shape for sounds like 'uh' or 'r'
                     3 = shape for sounds like 'th' or 'sh'
                     4 = neutral shape for no sound
                     5 = shape for sounds like 'f' or 'v'
                     6 = shape for sounds like 'oy' or 'ao'

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.mouth_text
documentation:
Display text (scrolling as needed)
Args:
    text (str): text string to display

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.mouth_display
documentation:
Display images on faceplate. Currently supports images up to 16x8,
   or half the face. You can use the 'x' parameter to cover the other
   half of the faceplate.
Args:
    img_code (str): text string that encodes a black and white image
    x (int): x offset for image
    y (int): y offset for image
    refresh (bool): specify whether to clear the faceplate before
                    displaying the new image or not.
                    Useful if you'd like to display multiple images
                    on the faceplate at once.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.mouth_display_png
documentation:
Send an image to the enclosure.

Args:
    image_absolute_path (string): The absolute path of the image
    invert (bool): inverts the image being drawn.
    x (int): x offset for image
    y (int): y offset for image
    refresh (bool): specify whether to clear the faceplate before
                    displaying the new image or not.
                    Useful if you'd like to display muliple images
                    on the faceplate at once.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.weather_display
documentation:
Show a the temperature and a weather icon

Args:
    img_code (char): one of the following icon codes
                 0 = sunny
                 1 = partly cloudy
                 2 = cloudy
                 3 = light rain
                 4 = raining
                 5 = stormy
                 6 = snowing
                 7 = wind/mist
    temp (int): the temperature (either C or F, not indicated)

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.activate_mouth_events
documentation: Enable movement of the mouth with speech

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.deactivate_mouth_events
documentation: Disable movement of the mouth with speech

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.get_eyes_color
documentation:
Get the eye RGB color for all pixels
Returns:
   (list) pixels - list of (r,g,b) tuples for each eye pixel

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.enclosure.EnclosureAPI.get_eyes_pixel_color
documentation:
Get the RGB color for a specific eye pixel
Returns:
    (r,g,b) tuples for selected pixel

This API is intended to be used to interface with the hardware
that is running Mycroft.  It exposes all possible commands which
can be sent to a Mycroft enclosure implementation.

Different enclosure implementations may implement this differently
and/or may ignore certain API calls completely.  For example,
the eyes_color() API might be ignore on a Mycroft that uses simple
LEDs which only turn on/off, or not at all on an implementation
where there is no face at all.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.gui.GUIInterface.show_notification
documentation:
Display a Notification on homepage in the GUI.
Arguments:
    content (str): Main text content of a notification, Limited
    to two visual lines.
    duration (int): seconds to display notification for
    action (str): Callback to any event registered by the skill
    to perform a certain action when notification is clicked.
    noticetype (str):
        transient: 'Default' displays a notification with a timeout.
        sticky: displays a notification that sticks to the screen.
    style (str):
        info: 'Default' displays a notification with information styling
        warning: displays a notification with warning styling
        success: displays a notification with success styling
        error: displays a notification with error styling
    callback_data (dict): data dictionary available to use with action
Utils / Templates
backport - PR https://github.com/MycroftAI/mycroft-core/pull/2862

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.gui.GUIInterface.show_controlled_notification
documentation:
Display a controlled Notification in the GUI.
Arguments:
    content (str): Main text content of a notification, Limited
    to two visual lines.
    style (str):
        info: 'Default' displays a notification with information styling
        warning: displays a notification with warning styling
        success: displays a notification with success styling
        error: displays a notification with error styling

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.gui.GUIInterface.remove_controlled_notification
documentation: Remove a controlled Notification in the GUI.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.gui.GUIInterface.show_text
documentation:
Display a GUI page for viewing simple text.

Arguments:
    text (str): Main text content.  It will auto-paginate
    title (str): A title to display above the text content.
    override_idle (boolean, int):
        True: Takes over the resting page indefinitely
        (int): Delays resting page for the specified number of
               seconds.
    override_animations (boolean):
        True: Disables showing all platform skill animations.
        False: 'Default' always show animations.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.gui.GUIInterface._resolve_url
documentation:
Resolve a URL to a valid file path.

Args:
    url (str): URL or file path to resolve
    
Returns:
    str: Resolved absolute file path or original URL
    
Raises:
    ValueError: If url is None or empty

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.gui.GUIInterface.show_image
documentation:
Display a GUI page for viewing an image.

Arguments:
    url (str): Pointer to the image
    caption (str): A caption to show under the image
    title (str): A title to display above the image content
    fill (str): Fill type supports 'PreserveAspectFit',
    'PreserveAspectCrop', 'Stretch'
    background_color (str): A background color for
    the page in hex i.e. #000000
    override_idle (boolean, int):
        True: Takes over the resting page indefinitely
        (int): Delays resting page for the specified number of
               seconds.
    override_animations (boolean):
        True: Disables showing all platform skill animations.
        False: 'Default' always show animations.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.gui.GUIInterface.show_animated_image
documentation:
Display a GUI page for viewing an image.

Args:
    url (str): Pointer to the .gif image
    caption (str): A caption to show under the image
    title (str): A title to display above the image content
    fill (str): Fill type supports 'PreserveAspectFit',
    'PreserveAspectCrop', 'Stretch'
    background_color (str): A background color for
    the page in hex i.e. #000000
    override_idle (boolean, int):
        True: Takes over the resting page indefinitely
        (int): Delays resting page for the specified number of
               seconds.
    override_animations (boolean):
        True: Disables showing all platform skill animations.
        False: 'Default' always show animations.

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.gui.GUIInterface.show_html

Display an HTML page in the GUI.

Args:

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.gui.GUIInterface.release
documentation:
Signal that this skill is no longer using the GUI,
allow different platforms to properly handle this event.
Also calls self.clear() to reset the state variables
Platforms can close the window or go back to previous page

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.gui.GUIInterface.shutdown
documentation:
Shutdown gui interface.

Clear pages loaded through this interface and remove the bus events

Interface to the Graphical User Interface, allows interaction with
the mycroft-gui from anywhere

Values set in this class are synced to the GUI, accessible within QML
via the built-in sessionData mechanism.  For example, in Python you can
write in a skill:
    self.gui['temp'] = 33
    self.gui.show_page('Weather')
Then in the Weather.qml you'd access the temp via code such as:
    text: sessionData.time

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.ocp.ClassicAudioServiceInterface.queue
documentation:
Queue up a track to playing playlist.

Args:
    tracks: track uri or list of track uri's
            Each track can be added as a tuple with (uri, mime)
            to give a hint of the mime type to the system
    source_message: bus message that triggered this action

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.ocp.ClassicAudioServiceInterface.play
documentation:
Start playback.

Args:
    tracks: track uri or list of track uri's
            Each track can be added as a tuple with (uri, mime)
            to give a hint of the mime type to the system
    utterance: forward utterance for further processing by the
                audio service.
    repeat: if the playback should be looped
    source_message: bus message that triggered this action

it has been removed from ovos-audio with the move to ovos-media

classes.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.ocp.OCPAudioServiceInterface

methods.name: ovos_bus_client.apis.ocp.OCPAudioServiceInterface.play
documentation:
Start playback.

Args:
    tracks: track uri or list of track uri's
            Each track can be added as a tuple with (uri, mime)
            to give a hint of the mime type to the system
    utterance: forward utterance for further processing by the
                audio service.
    repeat: if the playback should be looped
